showcases.name: Cobblestone → Sand Factory
showcases.blurb: A windmill-and-engine gearbox drills cobblestone, mills it down through gravel into sand, and a placer sets the sand back down.
showcases.description: A complete production line. A drill chews through the cobblestone in front of it (as if fed by a cobblestone generator), and the rubble drops into a hopper and barrel. Power comes from two passive-ish sources side by side — a large windmill and an engine — whose rotation is gathered through a clutch and a cluster of gears, then carried down vertical shafts to a pair of stacked millstones. The first millstone grinds cobblestone into gravel; the second grinds that gravel into sand. A placer at the foot of the column takes the finished sand and sets it down as a real block in the world.

showcases.name: Engine → Fan
showcases.blurb: A fuel-burning engine spins a shaft up into a fan that blows a gust of air.
showcases.description: The engine burns fuel — coal, charcoal, a lava bucket — to produce rotational power, measured in Power. That power runs up the shaft (a shaft just transmits rotation along its axis) into the fan, which turns it back into motion: a directed gust that pushes entities away from its face. The more surplus power on the network, the stronger the push.

showcases.name: Engine → Millstone
showcases.blurb: A fuel-burning engine drives a vertical shaft down into a millstone that mills items.
showcases.description: A fuelled engine sends power down a vertical shaft into a millstone. The millstone is always a Y-axis machine — it draws power from directly above or below — and grinds items fed from an adjacent container into new materials according to its recipe set while its wheel spins. Stop feeding the engine fuel and the whole column winds down.

showcases.name: Generator → Drill
showcases.blurb: A redstone generator powers a drill that bores forward through solid stone.
showcases.description: The generator is a passive power source — it spins on its own (and redstone DISABLES it, rather than enabling it). Its rotation feeds through a clutch into a drill, which bores into whatever block sits in front of its bit, breaking it in stages. A clutch passes power straight through like a shaft, but a redstone signal disengages it — a quick way to stop the drill without unpowering the generator. Because rotation only couples along a block's spin axis, the three blocks sit in a line.

showcases.name: Generator → Fan
showcases.blurb: A redstone generator spins a shaft up into a fan that blows a gust of air — no fuel needed.
showcases.description: A passive generator spins on its own and drives a shaft up into a fan. Unlike the fuel-burning engine, the generator needs no upkeep — it runs continuously until a redstone signal disables it. The fan turns that rotation into a directed gust that pushes entities away from its face.

showcases.name: Generator → Reverser → Drawbridge
showcases.blurb: A generator drives a rotator hinge through a lever-switched reverser; a pulse swings the drawbridge.
showcases.description: The rotator is a hinge: on a redstone pulse it gathers the connected planks into a moving structure and swings them as one — a drawbridge. Power reaches it from the generator behind, but passes through a reverser first: a floor lever beside the reverser is switched on, so the swing runs the reversed direction. Unlike the spinning machines, this is the mechanism system at work — real blocks are lifted into display entities, rotated, and set back down at their new orientation.

showcases.name: Gearbox Corner
showcases.blurb: A generator's vertical power meshes through gears to turn a 90° corner and drive a fan sideways.
showcases.description: A shaft only carries rotation straight along its axis; gears turn the corner. Here the passive generator spins a vertical (Y) gear, which meshes with a second gear mounted at 90° (X) beside it — a bevel pair. The horizontal gear then drives a fan on its own axis, so power that entered going up leaves going sideways.

showcases.name: Windmill → Drill
showcases.blurb: A wall-mounted huge windmill spins on the wind and bores a drill straight through solid stone.
showcases.description: Windmills are passive sources — they never need fuel or redstone, they just turn. Mounted on a wall the blades spin in a vertical plane, and (unlike a floor windmill) they deliver power horizontally along that spin axis. This one drives a shaft into a drill that bores forward through stone. Bigger banners mean a bigger windmill and far more power.

showcases.name: Generator → Press
showcases.blurb: A generator drives a vertical shaft into a press whose piston bobs to squeeze a barrel's contents.
showcases.description: The press is a Y-axis machine like the millstone: it takes power from directly above and extracts juices, oils and dyes from items in the barrel it mounts on. A passive generator keeps its piston bobbing without any fuel. (This demo shows the motion; a running press needs items in the barrel to actually produce anything.)

showcases.name: Water Wheel → Fan
showcases.blurb: Falling water turns a paddle wheel, which spins a shaft into a fan that blows a gust of air.
showcases.description: A water wheel is a source powered by moving water — not a still pool, but a real current pushing its paddles. A stream falls past its rim and sets it turning, and that rotation runs down a shaft into a fan. No fuel, no redstone, just flow.
